learning_outcomes.description: Explain the differences between nanomaterials and their bulk counterparts in terms of properties and applications, and apply this understanding to the design of fabrication and characterisation strategies for high-performance nanomaterials.

Apply X-ray crystallography techniques to determine the microstructures of nanomaterials and evaluate experimental data against peer-reviewed literature to identify the phase of unknown materials and justify conclusions.

Apply scanning and transmission electron microscopy techniques to determine the microstructures of nanomaterials and interpret the resulting observations in relation to material behaviour and application.

Design, fabricate, and characterise functional nanomaterials, including porous gold nanostructures and magnetic nanocomposites, and analyse their optical and magnetic properties for practical applications.
Select appropriate magnetic and optical nanostructures to meet the requirements of designed applications.
Design strategies to enhance the mechanical properties and performance of selected nanocellulose for targeted applications.
Explain the physical concepts and phenomena underpinning nanocatalysts and describe their current and potential applications.

Describe the applications of porous carbon, semiconducting, and porous nanostructured materials in medicine, energy, agriculture, and the environment, and fabricate, characterise, and improve their performance for targeted applications.

Construct literature reviews through critical evaluation of existing research on nanomaterial systems, and communicate experimental outcomes and research findings using the norms and conventions of the discipline.

Collaboratively engage in nanomaterials fabrication and characterisation, demonstrating effective teamwork, technical problem-solving, and the ability to independently collect, analyse, and critically evaluate experimental and literature data to address challenges in nanomaterials manufacturing and applications.

assessments.assessment_title: Active class/Laboratory engagement and progress portfolio
assessments.task_description:
An engagement portfolio is a collection of evidence that demonstrates that you have been actively engaged with your course, particularly lecture classes and practical sessions. In the section of the portfolio devoted to class engagement tasks, you will be required to include information about tasks that you have completed individually but in collaboration with your peers. However, all sections of the portfolio, you should write reflections individually. This will allow you to reflect on your own learning and experiences, rather than just those of your team.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Critical analysis on selected published literature on nanomaterials, their characterisations and applications
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia
assessments.task_description:
This assessment mimics an international conference. Each student/group will be assigned (depending on the number of enrolments) a high-quality journal article on nanostructure manufacturing and will be tasked to prepare a scientific presentation based on the article. The course lecturers will provide published journal papers on different topics on nanomaterials manufacturing based on their learning materials. Based on the assigned paper, the students will prepare a presentation. Students individual/group will present a 10-minute with 3-minute Q&A.

The presentation and Q&A will take place during the seminar.

Specific details for the presentation will be available on Blackboard. 

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

 A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students must submit their slides online on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Development and characterisation of nanoporous gold film
assessments.task_description:
The project will be conducted over four sessions. The first session will involve the deposition of a mesoporous gold (Au) thin film. The subsequent three sessions will focus on characterisation of the film using X-ray diffraction (XRD), scanning electron microscopy (SEM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM). These activities will be delivered as in-person laboratory demonstrations. Students will be provided with the experimental data generated during the demonstrations and will be required to independently analyse the results and prepare an individual report. The report is due to be submitted 4:00pm on 14 September.

Although this project will be conducted in groups, each student is required to write an independent report.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam will comprehensively test knowledge learned during the course. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
